key_points: Electric current is the flow of electric charge through a conductor
The SI unit of electric current is ampere (A)
Potential difference is the work done to move a unit charge between two points
Resistance is the opposition offered by a conductor to the flow of current
Ohm’s law states that voltage is directly proportional to current when temperature remains constant
examples: Electric current flows through a wire when it is connected to a battery
An electric bulb glows when current passes through its filament
A switch is used to open or close an electric circuit
Copper wire is commonly used in circuits because it is a good conductor
exam_tips: Remember the basic definitions of current, potential difference and resistance
Learn the SI units of current, voltage and resistance
Practice simple numerical problems using Ohm’s law
Understand the basic components of an electric circuit
